MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Continued To Feb-11-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2017 Set For Trial On Aug-06-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-19-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NBT-ERI TAHOE, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant NBT-ERI Tahoe, LLC's demurrer to the second cause of action for an equitable lien in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Joie de Vivre Hospitality, LLC is overruled. JDV adequately alleges an equitable lien on the net proceeds of the sale of the hotel based on equitable principles. Nothing in the HMA necessarily precludes the creation of an equitable lien on the net proceeds of the sale of the hotel and creation of such a lien is consistent with well-established equitable principles of good faith limitations on discretionary contract rights and prevention of fraudulent transfers and unjust enrichment. A judge, sitting in equity as a trier of fact, could find that distribution of the net proceeds of the sale of the hotel to NBT's members at a time when NBT owes JDV monies per the HMA resulting in the inability of JDV to recover the monies owed to it runs afoul of those equitable principles and warrants the imposition of an equitable lien.

Order signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Lewis Gillian , CSR No:12355 , email: lgillian@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Continued To Oct-19-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT NBT-ERI TAHOE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to October 19, 2017 per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Staley, Esq. (415-398-6392) of Epstein, Englert, Staley & Coffey for Plaintiff/Moving party .
Michael Lieb, Esq. (310-281-6338)of Ervin Cohen & Jessup for Defendant/opposing party.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The court makes suggestions to the parties. Parties meet and confer in the hallway. After the meet and confer, parties read the agreements on the record. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentaive ruling with modification as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOIE DE VIVRE HOSPITALITY, LLC's Motion For Preliminary Injunction

Plaintiff Joie de Vivre Hospitality, LLC's motion for preliminary injunction is denied without prejudice subject to being re-filed based on existing facts. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) JDV has not shown that its asserted harm warrants the requested injunctive relief. "Normally, an injunction will not issue where only money is involved. The rationale is that there is no threat of irreparable harm, because monetary losses are compensable in damages. Moreover, statutes governing prejudgment attachment limit the conditions on which payment of money may be restrained." (Weil and Brown Book 9:510, citing Doyka v. Sup.Ct. (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 1134, 1136.) Moreover, JDV has not shown a likelihood to succeed on its claim. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

PLAINTIFF JOIE DE VIVRE HOSPITALITY, LLC's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER.

Plaintiff Joie de Vivre Hospitality, LLC's application for writ of attachment is denied without prejudice subject to being re-filed based on existing facts. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) JDV has not shown either the probable validity of its claim or the probable validity of the amount that it is likely to obtain as damages at trial.
